paper_title: Clumped Isotopes and Noble Gas Measurements in Stalagmites

methods.description: This method involves measuring carbonate clumped isotopes to determine paleotemperatures. The isotopes provide a proxy for temperature, with higher values indicating lower temperatures.

steps.description: Stalagmites were sampled along the growth axis for stable and clumped isotopes.
CO2 was extracted from 4-6 mg of speleothem calcite by overnight reaction with 105% phosphoric acid at 25°C.
steps.searchable_summary: extract CO2 from calcite using phosphoric acid

steps.description: The generated CO2 gas was purified using a helium-purged GC column.
steps.searchable_summary: purify CO2 gas using GC column

steps.description: Measurements were performed using a dual-inlet gas-source isotope ratio mass spectrometer.
Several standards were measured regularly for inter-laboratory comparison.

methods.description: This method examines the co-variation between 47 and δ18O isotopes to estimate equilibrium values, which can be used to reconstruct paleotemperatures or past drip-water δ18O values.

steps.description: Theoretical calculations predict δ18O values to increase and 47 to decrease in dissolved inorganic carbon.
Empirical study supports the theory of co-variation between δ18O and 47 in speleothems.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze empirical data for isotope co-variation

steps.description: Project data back to zero offset using co-variation slope and offset in either δ18O or 47.
steps.searchable_summary: project isotope data to zero offset

methods.description: This method involves measuring noble gas concentrations in fluid inclusions of stalagmites to determine noble gas temperatures (NGTs), which are used to infer past climate conditions.

steps.description: Cubes of 0.5-1.4g were sampled close to the growth axis of stalagmite BU1, and 1.0-2.7 g for stalagmite BU-UWE.
steps.searchable_summary: sample stalagmite cubes for noble gas analysis

steps.description: Cubes were placed in a stainless steel crusher, pumped overnight at room temperature while the crusher was heated, and then crushed.
steps.searchable_summary: crush stalagmite cubes to release gases

steps.description: The released water and noble gas amount was determined for each extraction step.
steps.searchable_summary: determine water and noble gas amounts

steps.description: Noble gases were purified and separated prior to sequential admission to a static sectorfield mass spectrometer.
steps.searchable_summary: purify and separate noble gases for mass spectrometry

steps.description: NGTs were determined by inverse modeling of the noble gas concentrations.
steps.searchable_summary: model noble gas concentrations to determine NGTs
